revision.text:
[[ja:布留遺跡]] {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 36| lat_sec = 00.8| lon_deg = 135| lon_min = 50| lon_sec = 43.5| label = Furu Ruins| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 220| caption={{Center|}}}} '''Furu Ruins''' (布留遺跡, ''Furu Iseki'') are a {{ill|multi-layered archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/multi-layered archaeological site|1=Complex archaeological sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97186758}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan, near the neighborhoods of Furu and Mishima. This site spans from the {{ill|Japanese Paleolithic period|ja|日本の旧石器時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Paleolithic period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本列島の旧石器時代|1=Japanese Paleolithic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q844172}} to the modern era[http://www.sankokan.jp/exhibition/permanent_exhibition/18-furu "Furu Ruins"] Tenri Sankokan Museum website.. It is a type site for early {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery of the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == Overview == Located on the alluvial fan of the Furu River in the eastern {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, the ruins cover an area of 2 kilometers north-to-south and 2 kilometers east-to-west, centering around the {{ill|Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|ja|天理教本部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504165|1=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters}}{{Cite book|author=Tenri University Sankokan Museum|year=2001|title=Tenri Sankokan Museum Permanent Exhibition Catalog|pages=98}}. To the north lies the Ishigami-Toyota Kofun Cluster, to the south the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qid=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}, and to the east the {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|qid=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}. The site has been primarily investigated by {{ill|Tenri University|en|Tenri University|qid=Q4387971|lt=Tenri University|1=Tenri University}}. Settlements existed intermittently from the early to late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}, with excavations revealing deep bowls and other {{ill|Jōmon pottery|en|Jōmon pottery|qid=Q4390366|lt=Jōmon pottery|1=Jōmon pottery}}. By the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, the site became home to a large and continuous settlement. During the Kofun period, the area featured aristocratic residences, workshops, and {{ill|ritual sites|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual sites|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} employing {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (clay figures). Finds include Haji pottery, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, Korean-style pottery, sword fittings, and haniwa{{Cite book|author=Tenri University Sankokan Museum|year=2001|title=Tenri Sankokan Museum Permanent Exhibition Catalog|pages=98–101}}. The site is notable for the "Furu-style pottery," a hallmark of early Kofun period ceramics (late 3rd to early 4th century CE). This pottery is a key reference for chronological typology[http://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_furusikidoki.html "Furu-style Pottery"] Tenri Sankokan Museum website.. Additionally, middle-period Jōmon pottery styles such as Tenri C and late-period styles such as Tenri K have been defined based on excavations at the site. These names were chosen to avoid confusion with the term "Furu-style pottery." Artifacts from the site are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|en|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|qid=Q17229261|lt=Tenri University Sankokan Museum|1=Tenri University Sankōkan Museum}}. File:布留遺跡 堂垣内地区 出土品.JPG|Excavated items from the Dougakuchi District (origin of the "Furu-style" pottery name).
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Tenri University Sankokan Museum.}} == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Tenri University Sankokan Museum|year=2001|title=Tenri Sankokan Museum Permanent Exhibition Catalog|pages=98–101}} * {{Cite book|author=Hino Hiroshi|year=2019|title=Furu Ruins: An Aristocratic Settlement of the Mononobe Clan (Series: Learning from Ruins 140)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=9784787719409}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qid=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|en|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|qid=Q17229261|lt=Tenri University Sankokan Museum|1=Tenri University Sankōkan Museum}} == External Links == [http://www.sankokan.jp/exhibition/permanent_exhibition/18-furu "Furu Ruins"] Tenri Sankokan Museum website. {{Coord|34|36|00.8|N|135|50|43.5|E|region:JP-29|display=title}} {{History-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nara}} [[File:布留遺跡出土 埴輪・土器.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Haniwa and earthenware excavated from the Furu Ruins}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|en|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|qid=Q17229261|lt=Tenri University Sankokan Museum|1=Tenri University Sankōkan Museum}}.}}]] {{translated page|ja|布留遺跡|version=101667015|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:布留遺跡]] {{wikidata link|Q55539485}} [[Category:Composite archaeological sites]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
